message:
fix: patch uring-fs for ARM64 compilation (*const i8 → *const libc::c_char)

On ARM64 targets, c_char is unsigned (u8) not signed (i8), causing
uring-fs to fail compilation. This adds a local crate patch that fixes
the type mismatch.

Key changes:
- Add patches/uring-fs/ with corrected type signatures using
  *const libc::c_char instead of *const i8
- Add [patch.crates-io] section in Cargo.toml pointing to local patch
- Update Cargo.lock to reflect the patched local dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -193,3 +193,8 @@ single_match = "allow"
 len_zero = "allow"
 len_without_is_empty = "allow"
 doc_link_with_quotes = "allow"
+
+# Patch uring-fs to fix ARM64 compilation: *const i8 -> *const libc::c_char
+# (on ARM64, c_char is unsigned u8, not signed i8)
+[patch.crates-io]
+uring-fs = { path = "patches/uring-fs" }

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+[package]
+edition = "2021"
+name = "uring-fs"
+version = "1.4.0"
+authors = ["foxcirc"]
+description = "Truly asynchronous file operations using io-uring. Supports any async runtime. Linux only."
+license = "MIT"
+
+[dependencies.io-uring]
+version = "0.6.*"
+
+[dependencies.libc]
+version = "0.2.98"
+default-features = false

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!!! Hello, this is awesome !!
\ No newline at end of file
